summary: Test-only branch (three .test.goal regression pins for error.handle recovery-value semantics + a todos.md reconciliation by architect). No production C# changed, no new modules, no new API surface. One doc-hygiene gap found and fixed: the 2026-04-27 todo entry in Documentation/Runtime2/todos.md described exactly the work this branch shipped but lacked the resolution-marker its peer entries had. Marked resolved with pointers to the three test files, handle.cs line refs, and the auditor's minor caveat. All other doc surfaces (good_to_know.md:176 on ErrorOrder=GoalFirst, debug.md:296-307 on %!callStack.Current.Handled%, callbacks.md:108 on error.App back-ref) already cover the behavior these tests pin — no drift. The .test.goal files carry strong inline / comments naming the exact handle.cs line ranges; no further example or architecture doc work needed.

changes.description: Marked the 2026-04-27 'PLang tests for error.handle recovery-value path' entry ✅ RESOLVED 2026-05-11 (runtime2-foundation-verify stage 6). Added short close-out paragraph listing the three Tests/Errors/*.test.goal files with handle.cs line refs and the auditor's minor caveat (Test 3 can't distinguish 'return last' from 'return Ok()' under variable.set — defer-with-consumer). Original entry preserved under '### Original entry (archived)' to match the convention used by the resolved entries above it.

findings.issue: The 2026-04-27 'PLang tests for error.handle recovery-value path' entry was the exact work shipped on this branch but had no ✅ RESOLVED marker, unlike its peer entries (2026-04-24 lazy-generator OBP, 2026-04-27 CallStack wiring) which use the marker convention.
findings.detail: Resolution header + close-out paragraph added; original entry archived in place.
